title: Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Madhugiri and Koratagere Taluk of Tumkur in the state of Karnataka.
overview.description: Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Madhugiri and Koratagere Taluk of Tumkur in the state of Karnataka. It was declared a wildlife sanctuary under Section 36-A of the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972. It is inhabited by leopards, sloth bears, wild boars, Indian foxes, wolves, among other animals. Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary is a conservation reserve for sloth bear.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Yoganarasimha Temple, Devarayanadurga
topPlaces.description: Yoganarsimha Temple is a Hindu Temple located in hill station Devarayanadurga near Tumkur in the state of Karnataka in India. Situated on the hilltop, surrounded by other hills and forests, the temple is dedicated to God Narsimha and Lakshmi.

topPlaces.name: Devarayanadurga
topPlaces.description: Devarayanadurga is a temple town and hill station located in the district of Tumakuru in the state of Karnataka in India. The rocky hills are surrounded by forest and the hilltops are dotted with several temples including the Yoganarasimha and the Bhoganarasimha temples.It has an altitude of 1204 metres.

topPlaces.name: Siddaganga Matha
topPlaces.description: Siddaganga Matha also known as Siddaganga Kshetra, is a Lingayata monastery, with an attached educational institution. The matha was established by Sri Haradanahalli Gosala Siddeshwara Swamigalu in the 15th century at a village near Tumkur in the Karnataka state of India.

topPlaces.name: Mandaragiri
topPlaces.description: Mandaragiri or Basadi-betta is a Jain pilgrimage located on Mandaragiri Hill in Tumkur district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Hikkal
topPlaces.description: Hikkal is a hill in Urdigere Taluk, Tumkur District, and Karnataka State, India. It is approximately 16 km from the district's main city of Tumkur, and 70 km away from the capital city Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Aagam Mandir, Tumkur
topPlaces.description: Aagam Mandir (Aagam Temple) is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple in Tumakuru Karnataka. It is located 65 km from Bangalore, India, by road on Tumkur road.

topPlaces.name: Kote Anjaneya
topPlaces.description: Kote Anjaneya is a temple of Hanuman located in Tumkur, Karnataka. The temple has a 75 feet tall Hanuman statue at the entrance which was inaugurated in May 2005.

items.note: Yoganarsimha Temple is a Hindu Temple located in hill station Devarayanadurga near Tumkur in the state of Karnataka in India. Situated on the hilltop, surrounded by other hills and forests, the temple is dedicated to God Narsimha and Lakshmi.

Devarayanadurga is a temple town and hill station located in the district of Tumakuru in the state of Karnataka in India. The rocky hills are surrounded by forest and the hilltops are dotted with several temples including the Yoganarasimha and the Bhoganarasimha temples.It has an altitude of 1204 metres.

Siddaganga Matha also known as Siddaganga Kshetra, is a Lingayata monastery, with an attached educational institution. The matha was established by Sri Haradanahalli Gosala Siddeshwara Swamigalu in the 15th century at a village near Tumkur in the Karnataka state of India.
Mandaragiri or Basadi-betta is a Jain pilgrimage located on Mandaragiri Hill in Tumkur district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

Aagam Mandir (Aagam Temple) is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple in Tumakuru Karnataka. It is located 65 km from Bangalore, India, by road on Tumkur road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (60 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (61 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thimlapura Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yoganarasimha Temple, Devarayanadurga, Devarayanadurga, Siddaganga Matha, Mandaragiri, Hikkal.
